summary: Code review of RichTextEditor, ColorPicker, ImageEditor, ThreadPage, ProfilePage. UI playwright testing skipped due to token constraints - main agent should self-test.

ui_bugs.issue: When gradient is applied to textarea background, text becomes nearly unreadable. Gradient is set on background while text color remains #e8d9c0 - poor contrast. Solid color sets text color but gradient does not provide a text-readability fallback.
Button label/icon styling collapses when gradient applied since button background uses textColor.gradient - text 'A' may become invisible.
No data-testid on Font button, Color button, Bold/Italic buttons, Emoji button, or font items - hard to automate testing.
#f39c12 appears twice in presetColors array (duplicate React keys would warn).

Canvas uses img.crossOrigin='anonymous' but image URLs are from same backend - may still taint canvas if CORS headers not set. toDataURL() will throw SecurityError when canvas is tainted, breaking Save.

Initial position is (0,0) but no centering - large images render with top-left at canvas origin, may appear off-canvas at high scale. Drag uses raw clientX/Y without canvas-relative coords - position drift expected.
No data-testid on scale slider, save button, cancel button - hard to automate.
canvas.toDataURL produces large base64 strings sent as image_url in FormData. Backend may reject due to size; also persists base64 in DB. Should re-upload edited blob to /api/upload/image instead.

integration_issues.issue: Edited image saved as data URL (base64). Sending large base64 to backend in image_url field may exceed payload limits or break image rendering downstream.

richContent (font/color/gradient) is sent to backend but rendered posts do not apply font/color styling - PostItem renders body in default style, ignoring rich_content. Custom font/gradient is only visible while composing, never on saved posts.

issues: Gradient backgrounds make textarea text low-contrast
Font dropdown z-index 100 may be overlapped by emoji picker
Uses browser default color picker (input type=color) for start/end gradient colors instead of an RGB slider widget like the solid mode

action_items: CRITICAL: Re-upload edited canvas blob to /api/upload/image instead of using data URL, otherwise large base64 strings will bloat payloads and break image display
CRITICAL: rich_content (font/color/gradient) is never rendered on saved posts - PostItem/ProfilePostItem need to apply post.rich_content styles to body
HIGH: ImageEditor canvas.toDataURL may throw SecurityError if image is CORS-tainted; ensure backend serves uploaded images with proper Access-Control-Allow-Origin
HIGH: Center image on canvas initially; use canvas-relative drag coordinates
MEDIUM: Add data-testid to all RichTextEditor and ImageEditor interactive elements (font-btn, color-btn, bold-btn, italic-btn, emoji-btn, font-option, scale-slider, image-save, image-cancel)
MEDIUM: When applying gradient, set a readable text color override for textarea contents
LOW: Remove duplicate '#f39c12' from ColorPicker presetColors array
LOW: Duplicate 'Righteous', 'Lora', 'Crimson Text' entries in GOOGLE_FONTS array cause duplicate React keys
critical_code_review_comments: RichTextEditor.jsx 326 lines, ColorPicker.jsx 327 lines - acceptable size
ThreadPage.jsx 697 lines, ProfilePage.jsx 918 lines - exceed 700 line guideline; ProfilePage should be split (PostItem, Customize panel, Reaction picker as separate files)
Duplicate ReactionPicker logic between ThreadPage PostItem and ProfilePage ProfilePostItem - violates DRY (>50 lines duplicated). Extract to shared <ReactionPicker /> component
GOOGLE_FONTS array has duplicate entries (Righteous appears twice, Lora twice, Crimson Text twice) - causes React key warnings
rich_content sent to backend but never rendered on display - feature appears half-implemented
ImageEditor saves to data URL instead of re-uploading; consumes large memory/payload
context_for_next_testing_agent: Did code review only; no UI playwright run due to token budget. Priority items: (1) Verify rich_content rendering on saved posts (likely missing). (2) Verify ImageEditor save flow end-to-end - canvas toDataURL might fail with CORS-tainted image or produce huge payloads. (3) Add data-testid to font/color/image editor controls. Login as testuser/password123 and test font dropdown opens, gradient apply, image upload->editor->save->visible in post.
rca of the issue: N/A - first iteration, code review surfaced design/integration gaps before runtime testing.
